cards.back: A type of unfairness or discrimination in AI systems, often due to the underlying data or algorithms used
A concept in AI ethics that aims to ensure all individuals have equal access to opportunities and are not unfairly disadvantaged by AI systems
A set of rules, laws, and guidelines established by governments to govern the development and use of AI in various industries, including financial services
The practice of providing clear explanations and justifications for AI-driven decisions, enabling users to understand the reasoning behind the output
The responsibility of organizations and individuals to ensure the accountability and reliability of AI systems, including their data and decision-making processes
